info.title: Findymail API documentation
info.description: This documentation aims to provide all the information you need to work with our API. Rate limit: all endpoints have a concurrent rate limit of 300 simultaneous requests, unless stated otherwise.

tags.name: Exclusion Lists
tags.description: APIs for managing excluded websites from Intellimatch searches

default.description: You can retrieve your token by visiting API page.

post.description: Uses one verifier credit on all attempted verification

get.summary: Get the list of contact lists

isShared.description: Enable/disable list sharing with the team

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the list.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Optional parameter. Get contacts from a specified list. If 0, no list used ("All contacts").

post.description: Find someone's email from name and company (website or name). Uses one finder credit if a verified email is found.

payload.description: (when using webhook_url) object POST method callback request triggered when webhook_url provided. See example response

webhook_url.description: Webhook URL that will receive the result as callback. If submitted, email search will happen in the background and notify this URL upon completion.

post.description:
Try finding a contact with a valid email at a given domain with a given role.
A contact is only returned if we found a valid email.

Due to the heavy processing involved (real-time search), this endpoint is limited to 5 concurrent requests (when used synchronously)
and async jobs can take up to 24 hours to be processed depending on our workload (usually sooner).

payload.description: (when using webhook_url) object POST method callback request triggered when webhook_url provided. See example response

roles.description: Target roles related to the given domain (max 3 roles)

webhook_url.description: Webhook URL that will receive the result as callback. If submitted, search will happen in the background and notify this URL upon completion.

post.description:
Find someone's email from a business profile URL. Uses one finder credit if a verified email is found.

This endpoint is limited to 30 concurrent requests (when used synchronously)

payload.description: (when using webhook_url) object POST method callback request triggered when webhook_url provided. See example response

linkedin_url.description: Person's business profile URL. (can be full URL "https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe" or username only "johndoe")

webhook_url.description: Webhook URL that will receive the result as callback. If submitted, email search will happen in the background and notify this URL upon completion.

get.description: Returns all exclusion lists the authenticated user has access to (owned lists and lists shared with their team)

post.description:
Creates a new exclusion list for the authenticated user.

Optionally share the list with the user's current team.

name: The name has already been taken.

example: The name has already been taken.

name.description: The name of the list (must be unique per user).

is_shared.description: Whether to share the list with the current team.

get.description:
Returns the details of a specific exclusion list (without domains).
To get domains, use the dedicated GET /api/intellimatch/domains?list_id={id} endpoint.
Users can only access lists they own or lists shared with their team.

put.description:
Updates an exclusion list's name and/or sharing status.

Only the list owner can update or change sharing settings.

example.error: You must be part of a team to share lists.

error.example: You must be part of a team to share lists.

name: The name has already been taken.

example: The name has already been taken.

name.description: The new name for the list (must be unique per user).

is_shared.description: Optional. Set to true to share with team, false to make private.

delete.description:
Deletes an exclusion list and all its associated domains.

Only the list owner can delete the list.

parameters.name: excludedDomainList_id
parameters.description: The ID of the excludedDomainList.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of excluded domains for the authenticated user.

Can be filtered by list_id to get domains from a specific list, otherwise returns the global exclusion list.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query to filter domains.

parameters.name: list_id
parameters.description: Filter domains by list ID.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of domains per page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number.

post.description:
Adds one or more domains to the user's exclusion list.
If list_id is provided, domains are added to that specific list.
For large batches (>50 domains), the first 50 are processed immediately
and the rest are queued for background processing.

domains.description: Array of domain names to exclude (max 10000).

list_id.description: Optional list ID to add domains to. If not provided, domains are added as global exclusions.

delete.description:
Removes one or more domains from the user's exclusion lists.

Only domains the user has permission to delete will be removed.

For domains in shared lists, the user must have removeDomains permission on the list.

For global domains (no list), the user must own the domain.

ids.description: Array of domain IDs to delete.

post.description:
Create an Intellimatch search task.

Intellimatch is Findymail’s intelligent company search tool that lets you build highly targeted lead lists using plain language queries.

Instead of manually applying filters or reviewing websites one by one, Intellimatch uses real-time semantic search to find the best-fit companies and contacts.

The API responds immediately with a `hash` identifying the task for polling completion status.

Please check https://help.findymail.com/en/article/what-is-supported-in-intellimatch-15s0u10/ for more information about what is supported or not.

hash.description: Unique hash to track the export progress and retrieve results.

query.description: The search query describing target companies.
query.example: SaaS companies in US with 50-200 employees

limit.description: The maximum number of companies to export (default: 100, max: 5000).

find_email.description: Whether to enrich contacts with email. (1 credit per email found) (default: false).

find_phone.description: Whether to enrich contacts with phone number (10 credits per phone number found) (default: false).

target_job_titles.description: Target job titles for contacts. Variations that have the same intent (eg. "CEO" and "Chief Executive Officer") are already handled. (default: ["CEO"]).

lead_list_id.description: Lead list ID to add contacts to.

add_to_exclusion_list.description: Add exported companies to exclusion list (default: false).

exclusion_list_id.description: Exclusion list ID where to add domains when add_to_exclusion_list is true.

exclusion_filter_list_ids.description: List of exclusion list IDs to filter results (default: [0] = global exclusion list, [-1] = no filter).

get.description:
Poll this endpoint to check if a given search is completed.

The response will contain a `status` field with one of the following values:
- `success`: Export is completed and ready to download
- `processing`: Export is currently being processed (includes progress information)
- `pending`: Export is queued but not yet started
- `failed`: Export has failed
- `not_found`: Export hash not found or expired

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: The export hash returned by the search endpoint.

schema.description: The export hash returned by the search endpoint.

status.description: The current status of the export.

progress.description: The progress percentage (only when status is "processing").

total_jobs.description: Total number of jobs (only when status is "processing").

processed_jobs.description: Number of processed jobs (only when status is "processing").

pending_jobs.description: Number of pending jobs (only when status is "processing").

failed_jobs.description: Number of failed jobs (only when status is "processing").

get.description: Retrieve paginated company and contact results from a completed search task.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: The hash returned by the search endpoint.

schema.description: The hash returned by the search endpoint.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number (default: 1).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Results per page (max: 500, default: 100).

example.message: Export not found or not ready yet

message.example: Export not found or not ready yet

post.description:
Find companies similar to a given seed domain.

Optionally filter results by same country or same company size, and exclude companies from specific exclusion lists.

Credit cost: 1 Finder credit per 10 results returned (rounded up).

example.message: We don't have country data for example.com yet. The same country filter is not available at the moment but will be soon.

message.example: We don't have country data for example.com yet. The same country filter is not available at the moment but will be soon.
message.description: Informational message when filters cannot be applied (only present when relevant).
Informational message when filters cannot be applied (only present when relevant).

example.message: We could not process this domain. Please try another one.

message.example: We could not process this domain. Please try another one.
message.description: Informational message when filters cannot be applied (only present when relevant).

seed: The seed must be a valid domain or URL. Given: not-a-domain

message.description: Informational message when filters cannot be applied (only present when relevant).

example: The seed must be a valid domain or URL. Given: not-a-domain

message.description: Informational message when filters cannot be applied (only present when relevant).

example.message: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

message.example: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.
message.description: Informational message when filters cannot be applied (only present when relevant).

seed.description: The seed company domain or URL.

same_country.description: Only return companies in the same country as the seed (default: false).

same_size.description: Only return companies in the same size range as the seed (default: false).

limit.description: Maximum number of companies to return (default: 100, max: 10000).

exclusion_list_ids.description: IDs of exclusion lists to filter out already-known companies.

post.description:
Find a business profile from an email address (work email or personal email).

Credit usage:
- Uses 1 finder credit if a profile is found (without profile data)
- Uses 2 finder credits if a profile is found with complete profile data (only if `with_profile` option enabled)

with_profile.description: optional Whether to return profile metadata (default: false)

post.description:
Retrieve company data using a company profile URL, name, or website domain.
At least one of these fields must be specified.

Consumes 1 Finder credit per successful response (only when company data is found).

example.error: One identifier is required: linkedin_url, domain, name

error.example: One identifier is required: linkedin_url, domain, name

post.description:
Find one or more employees using the company website and job title.

This endpoint uses 1 credit per found contact.

This endpoint does NOT return an email.

count.description: Number of contacts to return (max 5). Default 1.

post.description:
Find someone's phone number from a business profile URL. Uses 10 finder credits if a phone is found.

For legal reasons, requests for EU citizens will not return any result.

linkedin_url.description: Person's business profile URL. (can be full URL "https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe" or username only "johndoe")

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of signals from the authenticated user's monitors.
Supports filtering by signal_type, monitor_id, and date range.

parameters.name: signal_type
parameters.description: Filter by signal type. Accepted values: `keyword_mention`, `new_hire`, `job_change`, `post_engagement`.

schema.description: Filter by signal type. Accepted values: `keyword_mention`, `new_hire`, `job_change`, `post_engagement`.

parameters.name: monitor_id
parameters.description: Filter by a specific monitor ID (must be owned by the authenticated user).

schema.description: Filter by a specific monitor ID (must be owned by the authenticated user).

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter signals detected on or after this date (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

schema.description: Filter signals detected on or after this date (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter signals detected on or before this date (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

schema.description: Filter signals detected on or before this date (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: relevance_scores
parameters.description: Comma-separated AI relevance scores to include (0-5). Optionally combine with `monitor_id` to scope to a single monitor.

schema.description: Comma-separated AI relevance scores to include (0-5). Optionally combine with `monitor_id` to scope to a single monitor.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number (default: 1).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Results per page (max: 100, default: 50).

monitors.relevance_reasoning: Post discusses active hiring for sales roles

get.description:
Returns all monitors belonging to the authenticated user, ordered by most recent first.
Each monitor includes a match_count with the number of signals matched.

post.description: Creates a new signal monitor for the authenticated user.

keywords.description: Keywords to track (required for keyword_mention and post_engagement without post_url, max 5).

webhook_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL to receive webhook notifications when new signals match.

post_url.description: LinkedIn post URL to monitor engagement on (for post_engagement type).

engagement_types.description: Engagement types to track (required for post_engagement). Accepted values: `like`, `comment`.

lead_list_id.description: ID of a lead list to automatically add matched contacts to.

icp_filters.description: Optional ICP filter criteria to narrow signal matching.

industries.description: Industries to match (LinkedIn Industry Codes V2).

patch.description: Updates an existing monitor. The signal_type cannot be changed after creation.

keywords.description: Keywords to track (required for keyword_mention, nullable otherwise, max 5).

webhook_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL to receive webhook notifications.

engagement_types.description: Engagement types to track (required for post_engagement). Accepted values: `like`, `comment`.

lead_list_id.description: ID of a lead list to automatically add matched contacts to.

industries.description: Industries to match (LinkedIn Industry Codes V2).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the monitor.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the signal.

get.description:
Returns the daily or monthly credit usage of the authenticated user.

Groups by day for periods shorter than 2 months, otherwise groups by month.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: optional The start date (incl.) for the timeline (YYYY-MM-DD format). Defaults to 30 days ago.

schema.description: optional The start date (incl.) for the timeline (YYYY-MM-DD format). Defaults to 30 days ago.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: optional The end date (incl.) for the timeline (YYYY-MM-DD format). Defaults to today.

schema.description: optional The end date (incl.) for the timeline (YYYY-MM-DD format). Defaults to today.

example.message: The from field must be a valid date.

from: The from field must be a valid date.

message.example: The from field must be a valid date.

example: The from field must be a valid date.

get.description:
Returns a summary of credit usage of all team members within a specific date range.

Only team owners can access this endpoint.

parameters.description: optional The end date (incl.) for the report (YYYY-MM-DD format). Defaults to today.

schema.description: optional The end date (incl.) for the report (YYYY-MM-DD format). Defaults to today.
